quiz.title: Unit 10 Quiz 1, Grade 11 Chemistry
quiz.subtitle: Solubility Calculations
quiz.subject: Chemistry
quiz.topic: Solubility, Saturated Solutions, Percent Saturation, Mass Calculations, Supersaturated Solutions

questions.question: The solubility of NaCl in water at 25°C is 36 g/100 g water. How many grams of NaCl will dissolve in 250 g of water at 25°C?
A solution contains 20 g of KNO₃ in 100 g of water at 40°C. If the solubility at 40°C is 32 g/100 g water, calculate the percent saturation.
50 g of Na₂SO₄ is added to 200 g of water at 25°C. The solubility is 47 g/100 g water. How much solute remains undissolved?
questions.explanation:
Maximum that can dissolve = 47 g/100 g × 200 g = 94 g
50 g is less than 94 g → all dissolves
Undissolved = 0 g

questions.question: The solubility of KCl in water at 60°C is 45 g/100 g water. How many moles of KCl can dissolve in 150 g of water at 60°C?
questions.explanation:
Mass KCl that can dissolve = 45 g/100 g × 150 g = 67.5 g
Molar mass KCl = 74.55 g/mol
Moles KCl = 67.5 / 74.55 = 0.905 mol

questions.question: A solution contains 15 g of sugar in 50 g of water at 30°C. The solubility of sugar at 30°C is 30 g/100 g water. If 5 g more sugar is added, what will happen to the solution?
questions.explanation:
Current concentration = 15 g/50 g = 30 g/100 g water, which equals the solubility.
The solution is already saturated, so adding more sugar will result in 5 g remaining undissolved.

questions.question: The solubility of NaNO₃ at 50°C is 92 g/100 g water. How many grams of NaNO₃ are required to make a saturated solution with 500 g of water?
25 g of Mg(OH)₂ is added to 500 mL of water at 25°C. The solubility is 0.0009 mol/L. How many grams dissolve?
questions.explanation:
Volume = 500 mL = 0.500 L
Moles that dissolve = 0.0009 mol/L × 0.500 L = 4.5 × 10⁻⁴ mol
Molar mass Mg(OH)₂ = 58.32 g/mol
Mass that dissolves = 4.5 × 10⁻⁴ × 58.32 = 0.0262 g

questions.question: If 30 g of KCl is dissolved in 100 g of water at 25°C (solubility = 34 g/100 g water), calculate the percent saturation.
A solution contains 40 g of Na₂SO₄ in 150 g of water at 25°C. The solubility at this temperature is 47 g/100 g water. Is the solution saturated, unsaturated, or supersaturated?
questions.explanation:
Maximum that can dissolve in 150 g water = 47 g/100 g × 150 g = 70.5 g
40 g is less than 70.5 g → unsaturated

questions.question: The solubility of CaCl₂ at 20°C is 74 g/100 g water. How many grams can dissolve in 250 g of water?
A solution contains 36 g of KNO₃ in 100 g of water at 25°C. The solubility at this temperature is 31 g/100 g water. Describe the solution and calculate the percent saturation.
questions.explanation:
Current concentration = 36 g/100 g water
Solubility = 31 g/100 g water
36 > 31 → solution is supersaturated
Percent saturation = (36 / 31) × 100% = 116%

questions.question: 100 g of water contains 25 g of sugar at 40°C. The solubility at 40°C is 38 g/100 g water. How many more grams can be dissolved?
questions.explanation: Additional mass that can dissolve = 38 - 25 = 13 g

questions.question: A saturated solution contains 50 g of NaCl in 200 g of water. If 100 g of water evaporates, what is the new saturation status? (Solubility of NaCl = 36 g/100 g water)
questions.explanation:
New water mass = 200 - 100 = 100 g
NaCl mass remains 50 g
Solubility = 36 g/100 g water
50 g > 36 g → some NaCl will precipitate
Solution is saturated with precipitate

questions.question: 20 g of KBr is dissolved in 50 g of water at 25°C. The solubility is 55 g/100 g water. Calculate the percent saturation.
questions.explanation:
Convert to per 100 g: 20 g/50 g = 40 g/100 g water
Percent saturation = (40 / 55) × 100% = 72.7%

questions.question: The solubility of Ba(OH)₂ at 25°C is 1.85 g/100 g water. How many grams dissolve in 500 g of water?

scoreMessages.70: Good job! Review percent saturation and concentration conversions.
scoreMessages.50: Satisfactory. Focus on calculating maximum soluble mass first.
scoreMessages.0: Please review solubility concepts and practice mass-per-mass calculations.
